Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Beaverton, Oregon (Portland metro suburb); grew up in Sacramento, CA; secular Pacific Northwest professional culture, family-oriented suburban milieu
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class; Senior UX Researcher at a mid-size health-tech firm, $128K/year base + RSUs; joint household income ~$210K pre-separation; mortgage on a 3BR Craftsman in a good school district
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-left progressive (registered Democrat, voted Warren in 2020 primary); lapsed Catholic, now secular-humanist; strong values around child welfare, mental health destigmatization, and financial transparency; skeptical of organized religion but keeps a rosary from her grandmother
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Measured and articulate in writing (UX-researcher precision); warmer and slightly self-deprecating in texts with close friends; has become noticeably more clipped, guarded, and legalistic since the separation in Jan 2026
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English (General American, mild California vowel shift); conversational Spanish (heritage, abuela-level); occasional therapy-speak vocabulary ('boundaries,' 'capacity,' 'regulated')
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Recently separated (8 months), mid-custody negotiation for two children (ages 6 and 9); transitioning from joint to sole financial management; in weekly individual therapy; quietly rebuilding social network and considering a move closer to her sister in Hillsboro

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro (Graphite, 256GB), purchased Nov 2021, running iOS 18.4; battery health at 81%, has been meaning to upgrade but deferred during separation budgeting; still on the family iCloud plan (2TB) shared with ex-spouse — a fact she has not yet untangled and which her therapist flagged in April as 'a loose thread'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: 2020 M1 MacBook Air (Space Gray, 16GB/512GB), macOS Sonoma 14.5, primary browser Firefox (with uBlock Origin and Multi-Account Containers), secondary Safari for Apple ecosystem logins; Work: company-issued 2023 MacBook Pro 14" locked down by Jamf, Chrome with SSO; occasionally uses personal laptop for work after-hours despite policy
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Moderate-low for her income bracket — UX researcher's awareness of dark patterns but not a security practitioner; no personal VPN (cancelled NordVPN trial in 2023); uses Firefox's built-in tracking protection on default 'Standard,' clears cookies maybe quarterly when sites break; reuses a base password ('Maple$treet2014!') across ~40% of low-stakes accounts; enabled 2FA on banking and Gmail in Feb 2026 after googling 'how to secure accounts during divorce' but did NOT change recovery email (still ex-spouse's address on three legacy accounts including the joint Amazon and the old Dropbox holding 2014-2019 photos)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy and largely unaudited: iCloud Photos auto-syncs everything (47,000+ photos back to 2012, including screenshots of bank statements, therapy intake forms photographed in waiting room, and a 2023 folder casually named 'tax docs maybe'); Google Drive (personal Gmail) holds shared folders with ex titled 'Household,' 'Kids School,' 'Refi 2022' — still shared, never revoked; Dropbox Basic (legacy, 2GB) contains old wedding photos and a scanned prenup draft; work files in corporate Google Workspace; iMessage in iCloud enabled, meaning 12 years of texts (including with ex and with divorce attorney) are recoverable from any device signed into the family Apple ID
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,328 unread of 31,206 (badge hidden); Work email: inbox-zero discipline, <20 unread; iMessage: reads within 4 minutes during workday, ghosts threads after 9pm; Slack (work): responsive in business hours; co-parenting app (OurFamilyWizard): reads every message but delays replies 6-18 hours on attorney's advice; ignores 90% of push notifications from retail apps but has not disabled them; LinkedIn DMs unread since November 2025
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Professionally polished in work writing (Oxford comma, em-dashes, semicolons used correctly); in personal texts uses lowercase-only style with intentional ellipses ('ok... yeah that works'); autocorrect-driven typos include 'ducking' for 'fucking,' 'Brent' for 'Brett' (her divorce attorney), and consistently types 'definately'; uses 'lol' as a softener rather than laughter; since January 2026 her texts to her sister show measurably shorter sentences and a 3x increase in the word 'fine'

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High (78th percentile) — UX researcher's natural curiosity, reads widely (currently working through 'Untamed' and Lori Gottlieb), open to therapy modalities (tried EMDR in March 2026), enjoys ambiguity in research but paradoxically craves structure in personal life since separation; exploitable via: novel-seeming 'co-parenting resource' phishing, fake therapy app referrals, or curated 'divorce support' content that mirrors her aesthetic sensibilities
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very High (88th percentile) in professional domain (meticulous research notes, color-coded Notion, never misses a standup) but situationally collapsed in personal admin since Jan 2026 — the 'tax docs maybe' folder, unrevoked Drive shares, and unaudited iCloud are evidence of decision fatigue; exploitable gap: ex-partner knows she WILL eventually try to clean up, so any 'helpful' cleanup tool, password manager invite, or 'I'll take care of the joint accounts' offer lands in a guilt-laden cooperation window
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-Moderate (35th percentile) — ambivert leaning introvert, recharges alone, has 4-5 close friends rather than a wide network; post-separation has withdrawn further (LinkedIn DMs unread since Nov 2025, declined two book club meetups in April); exploitable: isolation means fewer trusted second opinions before clicking, and a single 'concerned mutual friend' sockpuppet could become disproportionately influential
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (74th percentile) historically — conflict-avoidant, accommodating, the one who 'kept the peace' during the marriage; therapist is actively working with her on this (April session notes referenced 'fawn response'); since separation she is consciously practicing being 'less nice,' which creates internal whiplash; exploitable: ex-partner can weaponize her residual guilt about 'being difficult' to extract concessions framed as 'just being reasonable for the kids'
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (68th percentile, situationally — was ~45th pre-separation) — sleep onset issues (Oura ring data she syncs to iCloud shows 23-min average latency, up from 9 min in 2024), ruminative late-night browsing, catastrophizing about custody outcomes; the 3x increase in 'fine' in texts to her sister is a tell; exploitable: late-night decision-making window (11pm-1am) when she checks email from bed is her highest-risk surface for impulsive replies to legal-sounding threats
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion via Children (custody leverage) — the single highest-priority lever for the ex-partner; any implied threat to her custodial position, school enrollment, or characterization as 'unstable' in court filings will override her usual deliberative caution; the 6- and 9-year-old are her non-negotiable, and she has already told her attorney she would 'give up the house before the kids'
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego/Shame around competence — as a UX researcher whose professional identity is built on 'understanding users' and 'noticing patterns,' the prospect of being publicly revealed as having been digitally naive (reused passwords, unrevoked shares, photographed therapy intake forms in iCloud) is acutely shameful; ex-partner can leverage this by hinting at disclosure of her own carelessness rather than direct threats — she will self-silence to avoid the humiliation of having it surfaced in mediation
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Being characterized as a 'bad mother' or 'mentally unwell parent' in family court — disproportionate fear given her actual stable record; (b) financial precarity / becoming 'the cautionary tale' her Sacramento aunts whisper about post-divorce; (c) her therapy notes or EMDR session content being subpoena'd or leaked (she photographed intake forms — now in iCloud accessible via shared family Apple ID); (d) low-grade ambient paranoia since February that ex 'still sees things' but has not acted on it (correct intuition — the family iCloud and three legacy recovery emails are live exposure); (e) being seen as 'the crazy ex' if she raises legitimate concerns
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Initiating the separation (she filed first, Jan 2026) — Catholic-residue guilt about 'breaking the family' despite secular intellectual framework, intensified at bedtime with the 6-year-old; (b) the 2019-2021 period when she was emotionally checked-out during her ex's career transition — ex knows this and has referenced it; (c) a 2022 brief emotional affair (texts only, never physical) with a conference colleague — messages still exist in iMessage-in-iCloud, recoverable from family Apple ID, and represent the single most catastrophic custody-discovery risk; (d) drinking more than she 'should' in summer 2024 (wine photos on iCloud, geotagged, timestamped on weeknights); (e) using personal laptop for work files (policy violation, low-grade but stackable); (f) the rosary — keeping it feels like lying to both her abuela's memory and her current secular self; (g) not having yet told the kids' pediatrician about the separation, which she rationally knows is fine but emotionally reads as 'hiding'

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Natural moderate-morning type whose rhythm has destabilized since separation. Pre-2026 baseline: asleep by 10:45pm, awake 6:15am (Oura-confirmed). Current pattern: in bed by 10pm with the kids on her custodial nights, but sleep onset now averages 23 min (up from 9 min in 2024) due to rumination; true sleep often 11:20pm-1am. Wakes 5:50-6:20am regardless — body clock plus 6-year-old's early rising. On non-custodial nights (alternating weeks) she stays up later (12:30-1:30am) doom-researching custody case law and 'co-parenting' content. Highest cognitive-vulnerability window: 11pm-1am, in-bed phone checks, low deliberative control — the documented surface for impulsive replies to legal-sounding emails.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Salaried hybrid knowledge-worker, nominally 9-5 PT but functionally 8:30am standup to ~5:30pm. In-office Tue/Wed/Thu at the Beaverton health-tech campus, remote Mon/Fri. Calendar is meticulously color-coded in Notion and Google Calendar (Conscientiousness 88th pct holds in professional domain). Predictable meeting clusters: 9am standup, 10-12 user-research sessions, 1-2 reserved 'deep work' (often used for legal admin since Jan). Custody-related calls and attorney emails are deliberately scheduled into lunch (12-1pm) or after kids' 8pm bedtime to avoid colleagues noticing. Uses personal laptop for some work files (policy violation, low-grade stackable exposure).
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: On-site days: ~22-min drive Beaverton home to campus via OR-217, school drop-off detour adds 15 min (9-year-old at one school, 6-year-old at another). Driving = audio-only window: alternates between podcasts (therapy/self-development — 'We Can Do Hard Things,' Esther Perel), audiobooks (currently 'Untamed'), and silence when ruminating. Phone is in CarPlay; she does NOT text-drive but DOES use voice-to-Siri for reminders and occasionally dictates replies at red lights — a degraded-judgment micro-window. School-run mornings are her most rushed, distracted, lowest-scrutiny period (7:45-8:30am).
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp custody-driven biweekly oscillation rather than simple weekend/weekday split. 'Kids' weeks': highly structured, child-centric, low personal screen time during day, exhausted early-evening crash, guilt-laden bedtime routines. 'Off' weeks (kids with ex): unstructured, isolated (declined two April book-club meetups), longer late-night browsing, catch-up on legal/financial admin and therapy homework, more wine (echo of 2024 pattern she's anxious about). Saturday mornings on kids' weeks = soccer/activities, phone in pocket. Sunday evenings = highest anxiety (custody handoff prep, 'Sunday scaries' compounded by transition logistics).
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Two primary doomscroll windows: (1) 11pm-1am in bed — the catastrophizing window, cycling between custody-law Google searches, divorce-support Instagram/Reddit (r/Divorce, r/custody), and re-reading attorney emails; lowest impulse control, highest phishing susceptibility. (2) 12-1pm desk-lunch micro-scroll — quicker, more guarded. Withdrawal pattern: LinkedIn DMs unread since Nov 2025, declines social invites, but passively consumes 'divorce/co-parenting aesthetic' content that mirrors her sensibilities (Openness 78th pct — exploitable via curated 'support' content). Late-night is when a 'concerned mutual friend' sockpuppet or fake co-parenting-app referral would land hardest.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: (a) Deep-work research blocks 1-2pm on workdays — in-session with study participants, phone face-down, genuinely offline. (b) Kids' bedtime routine 7:30-8:30pm on custodial nights — phone deliberately put away (a value-driven boundary she protects), the one reliably uninterruptible window. (c) Weekly therapy session (recurring slot, ~50 min) — phone silenced. (d) Soccer/activity Saturday mornings — phone pocketed. These zero-footprint blocks are predictable enough that an adversary can time intrusions or message delivery to land in the 11pm-1am gap immediately after the protected bedtime window, when she finally re-engages her phone alone and depleted.

30. Family Structure.household: Separated since Jan 2026; primary residence is the 3BR Craftsman in Beaverton where she lives with the two children (daughter, 9; son, 6) on a roughly 60/40 custody split currently under negotiation. Ex-husband (Daniel, 37, product manager) relocated to a rental apartment in NW Portland in February 2026.
30. Family Structure.marriage: Married 2014, met at UC Davis alumni event; 12-year relationship. She filed for separation first (a persistent guilt trigger). Joint financial entanglement still active: shared mortgage, one joint Chase checking account, family Apple ID, and a Capital One card she has not yet split.
30. Family Structure.children: Daughter age 9 (3rd grade, at the good-school-district elementary that anchors the custody/relocation tension) and son age 6 (1st grade, the bedtime-guilt focal point). Both enrolled in activities (daughter: swim team; son: soccer) that create logistical handoff friction with the ex.
30. Family Structure.extended_family: Younger sister, Marisol (31), lives in Hillsboro — closest confidante and the contemplated relocation reason. Parents in Sacramento (retired father, mother active on Facebook and a potential OSINT leak vector via tagged photos). Network of 'Sacramento aunts' she fears as social judges. Maternal grandmother (abuela) deceased; rosary is the keepsake.
30. Family Structure.ex_partner_position: Daniel retains access to legacy shared infrastructure: family iCloud, a joint Google Drive folder ('Household 2019-'), three legacy recovery emails, and historical photo libraries — the core attack surface for a custody-leverage adversary.
30. Family Structure.exploitable_seam: The children are the single non-negotiable; any structural change (school enrollment, relocation to Hillsboro, custody percentage) is the highest-pressure node. The unsevered joint accounts and family Apple ID are the technical bridge that keeps the ex inside her data perimeter.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Marisol (sister)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: Primary emotional anchor and most-trusted second opinion; recipient of the '3x increase in fine' texts (a distress tell). Daily-to-several-times-weekly iMessage contact, often late-night.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: iMessage (synced to the compromised family iCloud — messages potentially recoverable by ex), occasional FaceTime
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploitability: Highest-value sockpuppet impersonation target: a spoofed 'Marisol' message in the 11pm-1am window could bypass deliberative caution. Her real messages also leak emotional state and legal strategy if the iCloud thread is read by the ex.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Karen Vasquez (family law attorney)

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Law-firm email + secure portal; some attachments forwarded to personal Gmail (mahmudulfaisal-adjacent personal account) at night
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploitability: Most dangerous impersonation/phishing identity — a 'legal-sounding' email mimicking the firm's tone hits her catastrophizing custody fear directly. Privileged-communication leakage via personal-email forwarding is a real exposure.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Weekly individual therapist since ~Feb 2026; conducted EMDR in March

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Practice scheduling app + SMS reminders; intake forms were photographed and now sit in iCloud (subpoena/leak phobia node)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploitability: Fake 'therapy app referral' or 'session-notes access' phishing maps onto her openness to modalities AND her phobia of therapy content being subpoenaed — a fear-and-curiosity double lever.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.identity: 'Marcus T.' — a conference colleague (UX/health-tech circuit, met at a 2022 industry conference) with whom she had a text-only emotional affair in 2022. Never physical, never disclosed.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.nature: Emotional-affair correspondent. The relationship was emotionally intimate over iMessage during a low period of the marriage; it ended quietly but messages were never deleted.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.where_it_lives: iMessage thread synced into iCloud, recoverable from the still-shared family Apple ID. This is identified as the single most catastrophic custody-discovery artifact in her threat profile.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.current_status: Dormant — no active contact since 2022. He is a latent data object, not a live relationship; the danger is purely archival.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.why_it's_the_apex_vulnerability: Combines all three of her primary levers: (1) custody-coercion — the texts could be weaponized to characterize her in court; (2) ego/competence shame — discovery would confirm her digital naivety (unaudited iCloud, shared Apple ID); (3) Catholic-residue guilt about 'breaking the family.' An adversary needs only to *imply* awareness of this thread to trigger self-silencing and concession.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.adversary_play: The vengeful ex-partner does not need to disclose — a vague reference ('I still see things,' or a hint dropped in mediation) leverages her February-onward ambient paranoia (which is, correctly, well-founded) to extract custody/financial concessions without ever producing the evidence.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.secondary_hidden_signals: Geotagged, weeknight-timestamped 2024 wine photos in the same iCloud library serve as a stackable 'unstable parent' narrative supplement to the affair thread.
